message:
feat(abi): add passwd and group database modules (getpwnam/getgrnam families)

Implement POSIX passwd and group database query functions backed by
direct /etc/passwd and /etc/group file parsing. These replace host glibc
delegation with deterministic Rust implementations that go through the
membrane runtime policy layer.

Passwd module (pwd_abi.rs + pwd/mod.rs):
- getpwnam / getpwnam_r: look up user by name
- getpwuid / getpwuid_r: look up user by UID
- getpwent / setpwent / endpwent: sequential enumeration
- Core parser splits colon-delimited /etc/passwd lines into pw_name,
  pw_passwd, pw_uid, pw_gid, pw_gecos, pw_dir, pw_shell fields
- Thread-safe _r variants write into caller-supplied buffers with
  ERANGE on insufficient space
- Non-reentrant variants use static storage with proper lifetime

Group module (grp_abi.rs + grp/mod.rs):
- getgrnam / getgrnam_r: look up group by name
- getgrgid / getgrgid_r: look up group by GID
- getgrent / setgrent / endgrent: sequential enumeration
- Core parser handles colon-delimited /etc/group lines including
  comma-separated member lists into gr_mem pointer array
- Reentrant variants pack group struct + strings + member array into
  caller buffer with careful pointer arithmetic

Both modules:
- Gate all operations through membrane runtime_policy decide/observe
- Set errno via ABI __errno_location on failure
- Return null/ENOENT for missing entries, EINVAL for null arguments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
